title: be going to (do)
desc: 계획된 의도 또는 근거 있는 예측
examples: Today, we're going to learn Korean in a couple of years.

title: will / shall
examples: Well, there's definitely some cultural differences, I'll say.
Next time, I'll be able to learn a really good English language.
I'll be able to learn a really good English language language.

title: When I do / When I've done
examples: When you go to the US, I don't have anything like that.
But when you go to the gathering, how old are you?
Until you get to know them better.

title: can / could / be able to
examples: So if you're actually concerned about the person, you can say, is everything okay?
So regardless of your size, you know, all sizes and shapes and all different kinds of people can be beautiful.
Thirdly, you can think that this is really weird, but it's a compliment to the Korean people.
You can use three rotations like this.
Next time, I'll be able to learn a really good English language.

title: have to / must
examples: You don't have to do makeup, you don't have to do anything.
You don't have to do anything like that.
You don't have to do anything like that.

title: would (habit/conditional)
examples: Today, I think it would be nice to tell you that we're different from other Korean people.
If you're trying to give a compliment about somebody's face, I would just say you look beautiful or you look very handsome.
My face is small, but I don't think it would be good to comment on the appearance.
What would you say?

title: Can/Could/Would you...?
examples: What would you say?

title: If I do / If I did
examples: If you go to the US, it becomes much easier to take care of your body.
I was like, okay, because I didn't know if it was good or bad.
